behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local lint logic over template text, not a cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check" (can be exercised as one pure function).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint rule on template strings that include copied phase-instruction snippets and on clean controls; assert violations are raised only for leaked instruction content.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture merge). Unit lint alone can miss post-assembly leakage. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond local wiring. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior is about assembled prompt text from harness internals, not real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-assembly path with production-like fixtures/interpolation and assert the final rendered prompt has no phase-instruction leakage signatures.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a source-of-truth handoff across loader, file-backed skill context, and invocation wiring. Unit tests of any one module can miss hard-coded fallback paths. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem/module wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP: not required to prove source selection. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because this behavior targets provenance of context loading, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation with real skill/plugin file loading enabled; assert effective context originates from skill files (e.g., by sentinel content) and not from embedded phase rules in prompt template.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires harness variant selection + file resolution + prompt reuse path working together. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network behavior required beyond local file/module interactions. LLM-DEP: this behavior is run-configuration capability, not output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because the requirement is ability to run unchanged prompt across variants, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness with baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures while keeping identical user prompt text; assert both runs execute and only context source changes.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on real model behavior changing when skill guidance regresses; a stub can always return expected structure and hide the failure. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model would likely miss semantic degradation effects. MULTI-STEP: not clearly multiple model decisions. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one behavioral test outcome under same prompt, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test twice with identical prompt: baseline skill vs degraded skill section removed. Assert degraded variant fails structural behavior criteria while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug is between model-call wrapper, capture layer, and parser/extractor stages. Unit test of parser or capturer alone can miss pipeline order regressions. REAL-INFRA: local artifact write is enough; no real network/subprocess required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because correctness is capture-before-parse sequencing, independent of model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise invocation pipeline with controlled raw response, then assert diagnostic artifact contains exact pre-parse payload and is written before parser/extractor transforms are applied.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is assertion logic quality (structure-aware vs keyword-only), testable in local matcher/validator functions. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior itself. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is about assertion semantics, which can be validated directly with crafted fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion module crafted outputs where keywords exist but schema paths/role placement are wrong; assert structural checks fail and valid structured outputs pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requirement explicitly depends on live model output patterns and regression manifestation. MOCK-HIDE: mocked deterministic outputs can be tailored to pass both checks, hiding real drift. MULTI-STEP: single live execution criterion, not clearly chained agentic stages. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text says "Under live model execution," which disqualifies stub-only validation for real failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts known to produce borderline outputs; compare structural assertions vs keyword-only checks and assert structural suite fails on regressions that keyword checks incorrectly pass.
